headline: How The Western Alliance (WAL) Investment Narrative Is Shifting After Recent Credit Headlines
summary: Western Alliance Bancorporation’s fair value estimate has been trimmed from $102.67 to $98.50, a relatively small reset that still matters if you are tracking entry points and risk upside. Analysts are split on what this new target means, with some seeing the recent pullback and updated models as an opening for long term buyers, while others prefer to stay cautious until credit questions, including the $126m Point Bonita charge off, feel more contained. As you read on, you will see how to...

headline: Citi: Bank Isn’t Leaving Middle East and Reports About Strikes Are False
summary: Citi said Saturday it wasn’t planning to leave the Middle East and reports about damages to its offices and branches from attacks were false.  The bank continues to serve clients in the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain without interruption and has a contingency plan in place to ensure business continuity, it said in a statement.  Citi said that it has evacuated three buildings and temporarily closed some branches in the U.A.E. as part of efforts to prioritize employee safety.

headline: Kohl’s price target lowered to $14 from $20 at Citi
summary: Citi analyst Paul Lejuez lowered the firm’s price target on Kohl’s (KSS) to $14 from $20 and keeps a Neutral rating on the shares. The company missed sales and gross margin estimates in Q4, the analyst tells investors in a research note. Citi believes Kohl’s is not well positioned to handle macro volatility and the firm sees a balanced risk/reward at current share levels.
